description: Fix inaccuracies in CLI reference, configuration reference, and troubleshooting documentation pages
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix plugin remove examples and config set signature in CLI reference
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI reference examples that actually work when copy-pasted so that I can manage plugins without errors
acceptanceCriteria: cli.mdx plugin remove examples include the --confirm (or -y) flag so they actually perform the removal
cli.mdx config set signature shows value as optional: `opentabs config set <key> [value]` not `opentabs config set <key> <value>`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two issues in docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx:

1. Plugin remove examples (lines 521-527): Without --confirm (or -y), `opentabs plugin remove` prints a warning and exits code 1 without removing. See platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts lines 227-233. Add --confirm to the examples:
   ```bash
   opentabs plugin remove slack --confirm
   opentabs plugin remove opentabs-plugin-slack --confirm
   ```

2. Config set signature (line 573): Shown as `opentabs config set <key> <value>` but the value is optional (square brackets). See platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 543: registered as `config set <key> [value]`. Change to `opentabs config set <key> [value]`.

userStories.title: Fix configuration docs: --dev flag, --dangerously-skip-confirmation, and config set port
userStories.description: As a user, I want accurate configuration documentation so that I don't try commands that don't work
acceptanceCriteria: configuration.mdx clarifies that `--dev` is a server process argument (used when running the MCP server directly), not a flag on the `opentabs` CLI
configuration.mdx clarifies that `--dangerously-skip-confirmation` is a server process argument, not a flag on the `opentabs` CLI
cli.mdx config set port description accurately reflects that the port is resolved from --port flag or OPENTABS_PORT env var on the opentabs start command, not from config.json
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Three issues:

1. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx line 277: Says `OPENTABS_DEV` is 'Equivalent to the --dev CLI flag.' But --dev is only a process argument checked by platform/mcp-server/src/dev-mode.ts via process.argv.includes('--dev'). It's NOT a flag on `opentabs start`. Reword to: 'Equivalent to passing --dev when running the MCP server process directly (contributor workflow).'

2. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx line 237: Same issue with --dangerously-skip-confirmation. See platform/mcp-server/src/skip-confirmation.ts line 15. Reword similarly.

3. docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx lines 597-599: `config set port` writes to config.json but the server never reads it. The server resolves port from process.env['PORT'] set by opentabs start based on --port or OPENTABS_PORT. See platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts line 179 (parseConfigRecord does NOT read a port field). Either document this accurately (that the port is set via --port flag or env var, not config.json) or note that config set port is not currently effective.

userStories.title: Fix troubleshooting page: auth secret file location
userStories.description: As a user troubleshooting auth issues, I want to know the correct file location for the auth secret so that I can verify my configuration
acceptanceCriteria: troubleshooting.mdx references `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json` instead of `~/.opentabs/config.json` as the location of the auth secret
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx line 104 says: 'Authorization: Bearer <secret> must match the secret in ~/.opentabs/config.json'

The secret is stored in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json, not config.json. See platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts lines 367-375 — auth.json is the single source of truth. config.json does not contain a secret field (see OpentabsConfig interface at lines 40-51).

Change the reference to `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json`.
